overview:
Ryukyu Village is a theme park where you can experience the feeling of time-traveling back to the good old days of the Ryukyu Kingdom era in Okinawa!
Within the park, Ryukyu folk songs, traditional performing arts, and powerful Eisa dance shows are held daily, captivating visitors.
You can also tour sugar carts recreating the traditional scene of water buffalo pulling sugarcane for processing, as well as precious old houses designated as National Registered Tangible Cultural Properties, allowing you to experience Ryukyu's history and culture firsthand.




You can also enjoy diverse Okinawan cultural experiences such as painting shisa figurines and trying on Ryukyu costumes. With a restaurant on-site serving local specialties like Okinawan soba and taco rice, this well-equipped facility allows you to fully immerse yourself in the island's culinary culture.

title: Glover Garden Entry E-ticket＜Western-style architecture from the end of the Edo to Meiji period＞
overview: Nagasaki opened its port to foreigners along with Yokohama, Kobe, Hakodate, and Niigata. The Nagasaki Foreign Settlement was established there. Glover Garden continues to record history there. Three nationally designated important cultural properties , including the mansion of Scottish merchant Thomas Blake Glover, and precious traditional buildings scattered throughout Nagasaki City have been relocated and restored here.

description:
What is Glover Garden?
In 1858 (Ansei 5), the Edo Shogunate signed treaties of amity and commerce with five countries: America, the Netherlands, Russia, the UK, and France. Foreign merchants with dreams of a new Japan arrived. The Dutch trading post in Dejima, which had been in operation for 218 years since 1641 (Kan'ei 1), closed its doors, and Nagasaki, which opened its port to foreigners under the treaty, was born as a "foreign settlement." Western-style buildings covered with Japanese-style roof tiles stand on the hills overlooking Nagasaki Port, and foreigners from around the world live their daily lives there. Glover Garden is located here. This area is home to the homes of foreigners from the foreign settlement period, including that of the adventurous merchant Thomas Blake Glover, and Western-style buildings scattered throughout Nagasaki City. You can feel the history of Nagasaki from the end of the Edo period to the Meiji period here. Enjoy a special time surrounded by the aroma of history and culture, with stone pavements, stone steps, and a panoramic view of Nagasaki Port.

description:
“Art Fireworks” — a one-night story that weaves unforgettable emotion




What “Art Fireworks” delivers is the ultimate entertainment experience: nearly 50 minutes of nonstop fireworks choreographed to music.




Set to a lineup of beloved classics enjoyed across generations and genders, the fireworks bloom across the entire night sky to express not only the rhythm, tempo, and melody of the music, but the emotion in the lyrics and vocals. With launches calculated to 1/30 of a second, and delicate control of hang time and lingering glow, the production is truly master craftsmanship.




“Art Fireworks” also has cinematic pacing, with a beginning, development, twist, and conclusion — plus the full range of emotions. A high-quality story, precise direction, moving music, with the fireworks themselves as the “actors” come together to create a single work that stirs the hearts of all viewers.




This special night will be etched in your memory as a deeply moving experience that never fades.




Note: this website only sells the Nishishiba Area, Toshiba Area, and Lawn Free Seating Area (Upper Level) tickets.




About Lawn Free Seating Area (Upper Level):
Trees in the foreground may obstruct your view of low-flying fireworks. While you will be able to see most of the fireworks, the music may be harder to hear than from standard seats.

title: Kyoto–Kanazawa Hokuriku Shinkansen & Limited Express Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible express train tickets between Kyoto and Kanazawa — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Hokuriku Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Shinkansen between Kanazawa and Tsuruga Station, connecting to Kyoto with the Limited Express Thunderbird!







• For other Hokuriku Shinkansen routes click here
• For other bullet train routes and prices, see our check our Japan Shinkansen ticket page
• See the How it Works section for full details of ticket usage!
highlights: Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!
Get Hokuriku One-Way Ticket tickets at exclusive rates!
Flexible tickets valid up to 90 days after booking!
Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
important_info:
How to Use:
1: Choose the option for your direction of travel
2: Confirmation email will be sent to the appointed email address once the booking is made
3: The QR Code* voucher for your ticket is valid up to 90 days later
4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)
4: Use the physical ticket to pass through the automated ticket gate




Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"
• "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor) is a status of residence defined under Japanese immigration law
• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period
• You must present your passport to receive a pass
description:
The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line that serves some of Japan's beautiful, less-visited cities like Fukui, Toyama, and Kanazawa. Change trains at Tsuruga Station for the Limited Express Thunderbird, which connects the travel hubs of Osaka and Kyoto!




With a Kyoto-to-Kanazawa (or Kanazawa-to-Kyoto) Hokuriku One-Way Ticket for the Shinkansen and Thunderbird Express, you’ll cruise between cities on a ride like no other!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

title: 【100 yen discount】Hida Takayama Retro Museum Admission E-Ticket
overview: It is a popular spot in Hida Takayama that is visited by about 100,000 people a year! While walking through the streets that make you feel like you have traveled back in time to the 1940s to 1950s, you can actually see, take photos of, and play with figurines, magazines, posters, toys, candy store games, and retro Showa pachinko machines. An experiential museum that is very popular with families and young people! At the elementary school booth, you can try out school lunches such as soft noodles, fried bread, and milk makeup. Would you like to try a nostalgic school lunch from the Showa era?
highlights: Experience-based Showa Retro Museum!
Since it is an indoor facility, you can enjoy it even on rainy days!
The nostalgic townscape of the Showa era has been recreated.

description:
■Showa townscape
The Showa-era townscape street has toy stores, electronics shops, barber shops, etc., and the roof tiles and telephone poles are replicas of the old townscape.
■10 yen candy shop game
A 10 yen candy store game that was popular in candy stores in the 1960s and 1950s. They are installed throughout the building.
■School lunch experience
At the elementary school booth, you can experience a nostalgic school lunch from the Showa era. Exciting school lunch experiences such as fried bread, soft noodles, and milk make.
■Showa era barber shop
A reproduction of a barber shop from the Showa era. You can actually touch tools that are rarely seen these days.
There are also various other corners!

important_info:
.

.
Pre-school children aged 4 to 6 will be charged admission only at teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM. Please purchase a child ticket separately in advance on the official ticket site.
.
Children price still applies to primary school students at 12 years old. High school students price still applies to students who are 18 years old and older.
.
This ticket cannot be resold. A resold ticket will be invalid.
.

Please check the homepage for the opening hours before visiting.
（ https://www.teamlab.art/e/planets/ ）

.

.
This ticket can only be used at TeamLab Planets located in Toyosu and cannot be used at other TeamLab venues. Please pay attention to the correct venue.
.

Please bring a device that can display the “QR code” or print out the “QR code”.

.
You need to show your student card when you enter TeamLab Planets TOKYO DMM with a “Youth ticket” .
.
[Tokyo Subway Ticket]
・At the time of collecting a paper ticket, you will need a “QR code” issued after the reservation is completed.
Please bring a device that can display the “QR code” or print out the “QR code”.
*If you are unable to present the “QR code”, you may not use the voucher.
・Please note that we do not offer partial exchanges of tickets. Each purchase will have a unique QR code, even if multiple tickets are bought. When you scan the QR code on the Tokyo Metro or Toei Subway, all the tickets you purchase will be exchanged and valid starting from the day of exchange.
・If the magnetic field on your Tokyo Subway Ticket becomes damaged or weakened, it may not be usable at automatic ticket gates. Please avoid placing the ticket near objects that emit strong magnetic fields (such as smartphones, home appliances, magnetic fasteners on wallets, etc.).
.
In case of bad weather or other emergencies, the facilities will be temporarily closed. Please check the website of each facility before visiting.
.
[Notes of Purchasing TOKYO DMM Tickets]
- All prices include tax.
- Please be aware that you may have to wait 30 to 90 minutes before entering.
- Purchased tickets cannot be canceled or refunded.
- If you are entering with a "junior high/high school student" ticket, present your student ID.
- It is strictly prohibited to resell tickets, and tickets purchased through resale will not be accepted. The facility will not be held responsible for any such cases.
.
[Notes for visit teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM]
Tickets and Admission
Ticket and Admission
・Please arrive at the venue entrance in time for the admission date and time stated on your ticket and line up in the admission queue.
・The time stated on the ticket does not indicate the duration of your stay inside the facility.
・If you leave the waiting line, please ensure that someone from your group remains in line. Guests visiting alone should speak with nearby staff.
・The waiting line is outdoors. Please prepare appropriately for weather conditions at your own discretion.
・Strollers must be placed in the designated area before entering.
・Children under the age of 13 are not permitted to enter alone. They must enter with a guardian aged 18 or older. Including free children, one guardian aged 18 or older is required for every three children under the age of 13.
・Some artworks may not be experienced by guests who are pregnant due to safety considerations.
・Re-entry after admission is not permitted. Refunds cannot be issued after entry.
Inside the Facility
・In Water, guests will experience the artwork barefoot. Please remove your shoes before entering the locker area.
・Some artworks involve water and may cause you to get wet. Please remove socks or stockings in the locker area.
・Some areas may involve water reaching up to knee height even for adults.
・For artworks using water, chlorine is used and adjusted to maintain appropriate safety standards.
・Guests with small children are recommended to bring clothing that can get wet or a change of clothes.
・If a guest’s clothing becomes wet, staff will guide you to change clothes. For safety reasons, guests wearing wet clothing cannot proceed to the next artwork. If you do not have a change of clothes, towels are available so you can dry your clothes before proceeding to the next artwork.
※Please note that this is not a swimming area.
・Some floors have mirrored surfaces. Guests concerned about reflections may use rental half pants or waist cloths.
・Free half pants are available upon request. Sizes range from XS to 6L. Please return them when leaving.
・Some artworks cannot be experienced while wearing sandals that do not secure the heel or high-heeled footwear. Free rental shoes are available.
・Some areas contain strong light stimulation or dark environments.
・Guests visiting with children must not let go of their children’s hands as it may be dangerous.
・Detour routes are available for guests who wish to skip certain artworks, including those with disabilities or those accompanied by small children. Please speak with nearby staff if needed.
・Guests with babies may enter while using a baby carrier.
・Diaper changing tables are available in the restrooms. If you require nursing or rest, please use the first aid room and speak with nearby staff.
・During busy periods, staff may ask guests who have stayed in one area for a long time to move on.
【Drawing on the Water Surface Created by the Dance of Koi and People – Infinity】
・For safety and hygiene reasons, guests with an inseam of less than 40 cm cannot experience this artwork. However, they may participate if carried by a guardian (18 years or older) using a baby carrier or by holding them.
【Moss Garden of Resonating Microcosms – Solidified Light Color, Dusk to Dawn】
・This artwork can be viewed in light rain. However, it may be suspended during heavy rain accompanied by strong winds such as typhoons.
Regarding Personal Belongings
・Inside the facility there are dark corridors, unstable floors, and artworks involving water. For safety and hygiene reasons, bringing personal belongings into the artwork areas is prohibited. If you must carry necessary items due to special circumstances, please consult staff.
・Free lockers are available. After passing the entrance gate, please place your belongings in the locker area.
Locker size: Width 23 cm × Depth 34 cm × Height 37 cm
・Belongings exceeding the above size cannot be stored as there is no cloakroom service.
・Large items such as suitcases exceeding the above size will be secured with a wire lock at the stroller area.
Photography
・Photography and video recording are permitted, but please be considerate and avoid disturbing other guests.
・Flash photography is not allowed.
・Monopods, tripods, selfie sticks, or any photography support equipment longer than 30 cm are not permitted to be brought in or used.
For Guests with Disabilities
・Guests arriving in wheelchairs will be required to transfer to the wheelchair provided by teamLab Planets when entering the facility. Please note that these are standard wheelchairs without fixation devices.
※The number of in-house wheelchairs is limited.
※For senior carts with handles or electric wheelchairs that cannot be controlled to a speed of 2 km/h or less, transferring to the facility’s wheelchair is mandatory for safety and accident prevention.
・Guests entering with their own wheelchairs must attach tire covers.
・For artworks with mirrored floors, guests with a total weight exceeding 250 kg may be asked to refrain from viewing. Staff will guide you and determine whether viewing is possible depending on the floor conditions.
・For safety reasons, guests using wheelchairs may be guided through a detour route or may only view certain artworks from designated viewing areas. Please refer to the “Frequently Asked Questions about Wheelchairs” for details.
・Entry with a cane or prosthetic leg is permitted. Depending on the guest’s condition and the nature of the artwork, some artworks may not be accessible. Staff will explain the viewing route and usage rules before entry according to your situation.
・Guests who require assistance for mobility should visit with a companion.
・If you are accompanied by an assistance dog, it will be kept in a cage before entry.
Please Note if You Fall Under Any of the Following
・Those who have experienced seizures or loss of consciousness due to light stimulation
・Those with extreme fear of confined spaces, darkness, or heights
・Those experiencing palpitations or shortness of breath
・Those who are pregnant or may be pregnant
・Those carrying children
・Anyone with concerns about physical fitness or health condition
Prohibited Activities
・Entry by intoxicated persons or those with inappropriate behavior
・Eating, drinking, or smoking inside the facility
・Bringing dangerous items or items deemed inappropriate by management
・Re-entry after exiting through the exit gate
・Commercial photography or interviews without prior permission
・Accompanying any animals including assistance dogs
・Accompanying animals other than assistance dogs
Other Information
・The facility assumes no responsibility for injuries, losses, damages, or other troubles that occur during use of the facility.
※For details of the venue and additional notices, please check the official website https://planets.teamlab.art/tokyo/#notice
・The facility assumes no responsibility for the loss or theft of valuables.
・Media coverage or photography may take place without prior notice. If you do not consent to being photographed, please inform staff. This applies regardless of the purpose of the footage.
・Some artworks may not be available due to unforeseen circumstances.
※Please note that refunds will not be issued to guests who are asked to leave the facility at the discretion of management, including for the reasons stated above.
.
The expiration date of the voucher:
[teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM Admission Ticket]
It is valid only on the date and time specified at the time of booking.
[Tokyo Subway Ticket]
The paper ticket can be collected within 90 days from the date of purchase.
*The exchanged ticket will be valid for 6 months from the exchange date.
*The valid time will be printed on the ticket when you start using it to pass a ticket gate.
*If you enter the station and board the train within the valid time, you can still leave the station even if the valid time is passed.
*If the ticket is not used by the expiration date printed on it, it will become invalid.
*If the exchanged ticket cannot be used at the automated ticket gates, please inform a station attendant. After verification, a new ticket will be issued.
.
The trademark for QR Code is owned by DENSO WAVE INCORPORATED.

overview:
Kobe Minato Onsen Ren is a luxurious natural hot spring facility facing Kobe Port.




It offers a wide range of amenities including open-air natural hot springs sourced directly from the spring, stone saunas, outdoor and indoor pools that are open all year round, and restaurants. This resort for adults allows you to enjoy a high-quality relaxing experience along with beautiful night views.




Enjoy the ultimate relaxation with a convenient day visit.

title: Asano Kajiya Knife Making: Forge Your Own Samurai Knife with a Certified Swordsmith in Gifu
overview:
Book a samurai knife making experience with Rakuten Travel Experiences at the best rates online!* Make a Japanese knife at Asano Kajiya, the atelier of a genuine Japanese swordsmith. Based in Gifu Prefecture, Asano Taro is certified by the Japanese government, and one of the few artisans still practicing authentic Japanese sword forging today. Visit a Gifu sword making expert for the closest thing to forging your own knife in Japan in 1 day!




While you’re exploring Gifu Prefecture, don't miss the breathtaking ride up the Shinhotaka Ropeway!
highlights: Book online to secure one of the limited available dates at Asano Kajiya
You can bring your own materials for a more personalized experience
Learn 'kajiya,' the ancient art of the samurai blacksmith, with a certified artisan
Easily accessible at just about 30 minutes by bullet train from Kyoto
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points!
important_info:
*As of May 2025, our price of 41,000–58,000 JPY per participant and 5,000 JPY for spectators is equal or less than any other booking site. Please note direct bookings are not possible.



If you come up at a different time than you reserved, you will be charged an additional fee for the experience
We refer to the email from Rakuten to confirm your reservation time
We can accept only a limited number of people each month, so we recommend making your reservations early
The "Add-on for Spectators" option is not available for more people than will participate in the knife-making experience. (e.g., 1 person to experience, 3 visitors, etc.)
Please arrive at Gifu-Hashima Station via Tokaido Shinkansen before 08:50
You will be greeted at the North Exit by someone holding a sign reading "Make Your Own Samurai Knife"
If you cannot arrive before 8:50, please contact emergency contact number. Please arrive before 10:00 at the latest to participate from that point; after that time, it will be automatically canceled
There is always some risk of getting burnt, so please be very careful and follow all instructions; we cannot assume responsibility in case of injury
To participate during holiday seasons, a private charter booking is required: choose an option marked "Holiday Season" and the required add-on "Private Plan Upgrade"
— New Year holidays: December 28, 2024–January 3, 2025
— Golden Week holiday: April 29–May 5, 2025
We will close the workshop during August 3–24, 2025 due to overseas travel

*Please contact the host directly for any urgent matters, such as being late. (+81) 090-9944-1792 (ASANOKAJIYA)
what_to_bring:
Long-sleeved outfit
Long pants made of cotton
meeting_point: North Exit of Gifu Hashima Station on the Tokaido Shinkansen
venue_address: Gifu Hashima Station, 645-1 Fukujusho Hirakata, Hashima, Gifu Prefecture, Japan, 501-6257
schedule:
Sample Itinerary



08:50 — Pick-up at Gifu-Hashima Station*
09:00–12:00 — Forge the knife at the atelier
12:00–13:00 — Lunch
13:00–14:00 — Shape the knife at the atelier
14:00–15:00 — Complete the knife
15:00–15:30 — Experience ends; drop-off at Gifu-Hashima Station



*Please arrive at Gifu-Hashima Station by Tokaido Shinkansen before 08:50
cancellation:
No-show: You pay 100% of experience price
8 days before: You pay 100% of experience price
description:
Learning to make a knife usually takes about 10 years of practiced apprenticeship — making a katana or samurai sword takes even longer. But on this Japanese knife-making experience at Asano Kajiya you'll get a crash course in the basics in just a single day! Learn and experience the traditional craft of Japanese bladesmithing for yourself; then, when you're done, you can take your newly forged knife home with you.




You will use the same tools used for Japanese sword making to forge a knife in the morning. Take a lunch break at the smithy, then complete the activity by shaping the knife in the afternoon. Below is a sample itinerary for this experience:




08:50 — Pick-up at Gifu-Hashima Station


09:00–12:00 — Forge the knife at the atelier
12:00–13:00 — Lunch


13:00–14:00 — Shape the knife at the atelier


14:00–15:00 — Complete the knife
15:00–15:30 — Experience ends; drop-off at Gifu-Hashima Station




Your host will prepare a lunch for you. Please let us know if you have any dietary restrictions. If you would like to eat something special, you can bring it with you.




The number of lunch options purchased should be equal to or lower than the total number of participants as lunch is included in the price of the knife-making activity. Please book the lunch-only option for those who will not participate in the knife-making activity.



Transportation



Take the Shinkansen bullet train to Gifu-Hashima Station. A free pick-up service is available from Gifu-Hashima Station to transport you to the experience location. Please refrain from going direct to the venue, since we will meet all participants at the train station first.




Approximate times from major cities:



Kyoto: 30 minutes
Tokyo: 110 minutes
Nagoya: 10 minutes
Osaka: 50 minutes



If you're coming by car, exit highway at Meishin Highway Gifu-Hashima IC and drive for 3 minutes to the station. Be sure to contact us in advance if coming by car.

The traditional Yumomi technique of Kusatsu Hot Spring that cools down the hot source spring without diluting its effects. Adorable monkeys will challenge this Yumomi technique. Be happy and be impressed! What kind of story awaits? Do visit us to find out.

description:
At the Monkey Yumomi Show, you can enjoy a fun monkey show filled with playful interactions between the cast and the monkeys, learning about Kusatsu Onsen, laughter, and excitement. At Kapiyu, there are Osayu friends who came to liven up Kusatsu Hot spring. Please be healed by the capybara bathing, rabbits, and baby monkeys.




■1st Floor [Osayu] Monkey Yumomi show
On the first floor, there is a stage where the Monkey Yumomi show is performed. Please enjoy the Monkey Yumomi show which is a specialty of Kusatsu Hot Spring.




■2nd Floor [Kapiyu] Animal interaction area
The adorable capybaras are relaxing in the hot spring (they are not always in the hot spring! They will get dizzy from the heat). Be soothed by their appearance.

overview: The National Science Museum is a national science museum where visitors can enjoy and experience cutting-edge science and technology, from the latest technology to the exploration of the global environment, the universe, and the wonders of life.
highlights: Explore how the "world" around us works, including the universe, the global environment, and the life that is nurtured.
Latest scientific data and tools will help you to understand how life on Earth and the environment are connected to you.
Imagine what kind of society and life we want to live, and think about what ideas will help us realize our dreams.

title: Otaru Tenguyama Ropeway E-Ticket Voucher[E-Ticket]
overview:
At 532.4 meters above sea level, Otaru Mt.Tengu is the symbol of Otaru.




At the top of the mountain, there are five observation decks, a restaurant, a tengu statue that is said to grant wishes if you stroke its nose, a slide and other playground equipment, and other attractions such as the Tengu-no-Yakata, Tenguyama Shrine, and a chipmunk park (open from May to the end of September).




The observatory overlooks the Sea of Japan and the city, and is considered one of the “Three Greatest Night Views of Hokkaido” for its beautiful nightscape that looks like a scattering of jewels, and was awarded a star in the Michelin Green Guide Japon 2009.




At TENGUU CAFE, is the closest to the sky in Otaru, you can enjoy delicious food and beverages while admiring the spectacular view.
highlights: Enjoy the spectacular view of Otaru from the Tenguyama Ropeway!
Earn points when you book with your Rakuten account!

This ticket does not give priority boarding or reduce waiting time.

title: Bonsai Experience and Tour of Shunkaen Bonsai Museum Tour in Tokyo
overview:
Book a bonsai experience and tour at Shunkaen Bonsai Museum in Tokyo! Enjoy a short lesson where you can try working on one of the miniature trees at the museum, then explore this hidden sanctuary with a bonsai expert as your guide. After the experience, stay as long as you like to enjoy the museum.




Visitors can also buy a Bonsai Museum E-Tickets or Experience a Tea Ceremony or Wearing Kimono at Bonsai Museum or wearing kimono at the museum!
highlights: Discover the Shunkaen Bonsai Museum, a true hidden gem of Tokyo
Price includes museum admission: stay as long as you like after the tour
Reserve with a Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points for each booking
Have a hands-on experience working with bonsai, guided by an expert
important_info:
Please wear clothes that you don't mind getting dirty, and bring gloves if you are concerned about getting your hands dirty
There is parking space for 10 cars; large tour buses can also be parked here
what_to_bring: Please wear clothes that you don't mind getting dirty, and bring gloves if you are concerned about getting your hands dirty
meeting_point: Shunkaen Bonsai Museum
venue_address: 1-29-16 Shinbori, Edogawa-ku, Tokyo, Japan, 132-0001
schedule: Offered at 10:00 and 14:00 on Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday.
cancellation:
No-show: You pay 100% of experience price
2 days before: You pay 100% of experience price
description:
The Shunkaen Bonsai Museum opened in 2002 in Tokyo’s Edogawa Ward. Tucked away in a residential area, the museum offers a secluded atmosphere to enjoy bonsai in peace. But don’t let the name “museum” fool you — this is a place of living beauty!




In this activity, you will get to handle a bonsai yourself, and learn something about the great care and precision required to care for it.




After your bonsai experience, take a brief tour of the museum with your host, and learn about the miniature man-made forest that stands as a bubble of serenity in the frantic capital.




When the tour is done, spend as long as you wish in the musuem. Experience the splendor of these artful expressions of natural beauty and the harshness of nature hidden within it.




The beauty at Shunkaen Bonsai Museum is the fruit of Kunio Kobayashi, an award-winning bonsai master who has held many bonsai exhibitions in Japan and around the world.

This course takes place on Okinawa's main island.

The museum building is based on the motif of a “manor house,” a medieval aristocratic residence, creating an atmosphere as if you were in a foreign country. Inside the museum, there are large exhibition rooms such as “St. Michel's Church” with antique stained glass windows of the four archangels, Uriel, Raphael, Michel, and Gabriel, and “St. Mary's Chapel” surrounded by vivid stained glass and the beautiful sound of an antique music box. The “St. Michel Church” with antique stained glass windows of angels and the “St. Marys Chapel” surrounded by vivid stained glass windows and the beautiful sound of antique music boxes.

title: Book Kabira Bay Glass-Bottom Boat at Ishigaki Island in Okinawa [E-Ticket]
overview:
Book instant e-tickets for a Kabira Bay glass-bottom boat ride Kabira Marine Service with Rakuten to earn or use points for every ticket!




Embark on a journey from Ishigaki Island aboard a transparent-bottom vessel with Kabira Marine Service. Even kids and non-swimmers can marvel at the underwater wonders beneath the waves, without getting wet!
highlights: Experience the beautiful underwater world of Ishigaki Island from above the waves
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each ticket!
Enjoy various coral reefs and colorful tropical fish through the transparent floor
Take a 30-minute glass-bottom boat ride with an expert on Kabira Bay
